name: Breadcrumbs
library: Material UI (MUI) v9 ÔÇö Cartrack-themed via @karoo-ui/core
description: Breadcrumbs show where the operator is within the portal's hierarchy and let them step back up it ÔÇö from a single vehicle, to the vehicle list, to the fleet. They orient users in deep desktop navigation.
keyRule: Use breadcrumbs when content sits more than one level deep. They complement ÔÇö not replace ÔÇö the left navigation drawer. Breadcrumbs renders whatever `children` you pass (typically `Link`/`Typography` elements) with its own separators ÔÇö it does not take a data prop.

variants.name: Standard
variants.whenToUse: Show the full path for shallow hierarchies ÔÇö render one child per level.

variants.name: Collapsed
variants.whenToUse: `maxItems` triggers automatic collapse of the middle levels behind an expandable "ÔÇª" once the path exceeds it (default 8).

variants.name: Custom separator
variants.whenToUse: Pass a custom `separator` node (icon, different glyph) instead of the default "/".

anatomy.name: Crumb child
anatomy.description: each `children` element is an ancestor level ÔÇö typically a `Link` (navigates up) or plain `Typography` for the current page.

anatomy.name: Separator
anatomy.description: `separator` prop, default "/", rendered between each pair of children ÔÇö decorative, hidden from assistive tech.

anatomy.name: Current page
anatomy.description: the last child, conventionally rendered as non-link `Typography` with `aria-current="page"`.

anatomy.name: Collapse control
anatomy.description: when `maxItems` is exceeded, an expand button (labelled via `expandText`, default "Show path") reveals the hidden middle items.

behaviors.name: Navigation
behaviors.description: Each crumb except the last is conventionally a link that navigates to that ancestor; the current page is plain text ÔÇö Breadcrumbs doesn't enforce this, it's a usage convention.

behaviors.name: Collapse
behaviors.description: `itemsBeforeCollapse` (default 1) and `itemsAfterCollapse` (default 1) control how many crumbs stay visible around the collapsed "ÔÇª" when `maxItems` is exceeded.

behaviors.name: Truncation
behaviors.description: Long labels aren't truncated by Breadcrumbs itself ÔÇö wrap long crumb text with your own text-overflow/Tooltip handling.

states.treatment: on-surface-medium; underlines on hover (styling of the Link child, not Breadcrumbs itself).
middle crumbs replaced by an expand ellipsis button until clicked.

specs.value: Roboto 14px / 400 (semantic.typography.scale.body2 ÔÇö the app-wide default Typography variant); Breadcrumbs itself has no text styling, it inherits from its children
on-surface-medium ÔåÆ primary-dark on hover (styling applied to the Link child)

props.name: children
props.description: The full ancestor path as a list of elements (Link/Typography), ending with the current page. There is no `items` array prop ÔÇö Breadcrumbs renders children directly.

props.name: separator
props.description: Custom separator node between crumbs. Default "/".

props.name: maxItems
props.description: Max crumbs to show before collapsing the middle behind "ÔÇª". Default 8.

props.name: itemsBeforeCollapse
props.description: How many leading crumbs stay visible when collapsed. Default 1.

props.name: itemsAfterCollapse
props.description: How many trailing crumbs stay visible when collapsed. Default 1.

props.name: expandText
props.description: Accessible label for the collapse-expand button. Default 'Show path'.

doThis: Make the current page the non-link last crumb, with aria-current="page".
Let `maxItems` collapse very deep paths instead of hand-rolling truncation.
dontDoThis: Don't use breadcrumbs as the primary navigation.
Don't show a one-level trail (no value).
Don't make the current page a link to itself.
accessibility: Breadcrumbs renders a <nav aria-label="breadcrumb"> wrapping an <ol> automatically.
The current page should carry aria-current="page" (set it on your Typography child ÔÇö Breadcrumbs doesn't add it for you).
Separators are decorative (hidden from assistive tech) automatically.
Links are keyboard reachable with visible focus.

related.name: Tabs
related.why: For switching sibling views at the same level

related.name: PageHeader
related.why: Repurposed as the back-link on PageHeader's detail-page variant
